jokes.text: Bart, you should warn people this episode is very frightening. Maybe they'd rather listen to that War of the Worlds broadcast on N.P.R., hmm?
jokes.setup: Halloween anthology episode about to begin with scary stories
jokes.punchline: Suggesting NPR's boring radio drama as preferable alternative entertainment
jokes.explanation: Smart meta-joke that breaks fourth wall while making cultural commentary about NPR's reputation. Well-structured with economy.

jokes.text: Sorry, Homer. While you were daydreaming, we ate all the doughnuts. Well, there were a few left, but we chucked 'em at an old man for kicks.
jokes.setup: Homer missing out on donuts while daydreaming
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of elder abuse for entertainment
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that escalates beyond simple cruelty. Fits Lenny/Carl's casual callousness perfectly.

jokes.text: Damn buzzards! I ain't dead yet!
jokes.setup: Old man being pelted with donuts
jokes.punchline: His defiant response comparing tormentors to scavengers
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective payoff to the donut-throwing setup. Good economy.

jokes.text: Dear Homer, I.O.U. one emergency doughnut. Signed, Homer.
jokes.setup: Homer desperately looking for emergency donut
jokes.punchline: He owes himself a donut he clearly already ate
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - creating bureaucracy for his own gluttony. Excellent character integration and absurdist structure.

jokes.text: Bastard! He's always one step ahead.
jokes.setup: Homer discovering his own IOU
jokes.punchline: Treating his past self as a separate adversary
jokes.explanation: Brilliant extension of Homer's self-delusion. He genuinely sees past-Homer as an antagonist.

jokes.text: Flanders! You're the devil? It's always the one you least suspect.
jokes.setup: Devil appears to make soul deal with Homer
jokes.punchline: Revealing it's the overly-Christian neighbor
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion - the most religious character as Satan. Perfect ironic casting with Homer's oblivious commentary.

jokes.text: Now, many people offer to sell their souls without reflecting upon the grave ramifica-- Do you have a doughnut or not?
jokes.setup: Devil Ned beginning formal soul-selling speech
jokes.punchline: Homer interrupting with impatient demand
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer impatience cutting through dramatic moment. Perfect character moment.

jokes.text: Hmm. Who's that goat-legged fellow? I like the cut of his jib.
jokes.setup: Burns observing Devil Flanders
jokes.punchline: Casual admiration for Satan using nautical terminology
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns - treating Satan like a business prospect with his antiquated vocabulary.

jokes.text: Hey, wait, if I don't finish this last bite... you don't get my soul, do you? I'm smarter than the devil! I'm smarter than the dev--
jokes.setup: Homer about to finish soul-damning donut
jokes.punchline: Thinks he found loophole then immediately celebrates prematurely
jokes.explanation: Great Homer moment - brief intelligence followed by immediate self-sabotage. Classic structure.

jokes.text: Mmm! Forbidden doughnut.
jokes.setup: Homer eating the soul-damning donut at home
jokes.punchline: His signature food appreciation sound for something that will damn him
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer food reaction applied to supernatural damnation. Highly quotable callback.

jokes.text: Your wide behind won't save you this time.
jokes.setup: Devil Ned confronting Homer
jokes.punchline: Polite Flanders delivering harsh insult in gentle tone
jokes.explanation: Devil Flanders maintaining his speech patterns while being threatening. Good character integration.

jokes.text: Oh, you Americans with your 'due process' and 'fair trials.' This is always so much easier in Mexico.
jokes.setup: Bart demanding fair trial for Homer
jokes.punchline: Devil commenting on international legal systems
jokes.explanation: Dark political humor about justice systems. Well-structured with good economy.

jokes.text: So, you like doughnuts, eh? Well... have all the doughnuts in the world!
jokes.setup: Devil planning Homer's torment
jokes.punchline: Punishment that's actually Homer's dream come true
jokes.explanation: Classic ironic punishment setup - what should be torture becomes pleasure for Homer.

jokes.text: More. I don't understand it. James Coco went mad in 15 minutes.
jokes.setup: Homer continuing to enjoy infinite donuts
jokes.punchline: Devil's confusion with obscure celebrity reference
jokes.explanation: Clever obscure reference to overweight actor. Shows Devil's experience with gluttony punishment.

jokes.text: Mr. Simpson, don't you worry. I watched Matlock in a bar last night. The sound wasn't on, but I think I got the gist of it.
jokes.setup: Homer needing legal defense against Devil
jokes.punchline: Hutz's completely inadequate legal preparation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lionel Hutz - absurd confidence despite obvious incompetence. Highly quotable and character-specific.

jokes.text: But I'm not dead yet. In fact, I just wrote an article for Redbook.
jokes.setup: Nixon called to jury of the damned
jokes.punchline: Protesting he's alive with oddly specific magazine reference
jokes.explanation: Absurd detail about Nixon writing for women's magazine. Good structure and timing.

jokes.text: And the starting line of the 1976 Philadelphia Flyers.
jokes.setup: Devil listing historical villains for jury
jokes.punchline: Including notoriously violent hockey team
jokes.explanation: Brilliant sports reference - treating hockey goons as historical villains. Very specific cultural knowledge.

jokes.text: Arr! This chair be high, says I.
jokes.setup: Blackbeard getting last chair in hell's courtroom
jokes.punchline: Pirate's complaint about furniture height in pirate dialect
jokes.explanation: Simple character humor - pirate speaking in character about mundane complaint.

jokes.text: Which I delivered! And it was scrum-didilly-umptious.
jokes.setup: Devil Ned describing the donut he provided
jokes.punchline: Using his characteristic 'diddly' speech pattern as Satan
jokes.explanation: Perfect character consistency - even as Devil, Ned keeps his speech patterns. Excellent integration.

jokes.text: Webster's defines it as 'an agreement under the law which is unbreakable.' 'Which is unbreakable'!
jokes.setup: Hutz defending Homer's soul contract
jokes.punchline: Emphasizing the part that proves his client is doomed
jokes.explanation: Classic Hutz incompetence - accidentally making the prosecution's case stronger. Perfect lawyer character work.

jokes.text: Excuse me. I must use the restroom. [Door Slams] Uh, Mr. Hutz?
jokes.setup: Hutz realizing he's making things worse
jokes.punchline: Literally running away from his own case
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy beat showing Hutz's cowardice. Good structure and economy.

jokes.text: Wait a minute. You got married in an emergency room? Well, Homer ate the entire wedding cake by himself... before the wedding.
jokes.setup: Devil noticing unusual wedding photo location
jokes.punchline: Revealing Homer's gluttony ruined their wedding
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer backstory - his appetite creating medical emergencies. Good character integration.

jokes.text: Dear Marge, You have given me your hand in marriage. All I can give you in return is my soul... which I pledge to you forever.
jokes.setup: Homer seemingly doomed by unbreakable contract
jokes.punchline: His romantic wedding vow creating legal loophole
jokes.explanation: Excellent plot reversal - Homer's rare romantic moment saves him. Well-earned twist ending.

jokes.text: But let that ill-gotten doughnut be forever on your head.
jokes.setup: Devil Ned forced to release Homer's soul
jokes.punchline: Petty revenge curse creating physical donut hat
jokes.explanation: Visual gag setup with biblical language. Creates ongoing visual comedy element.

jokes.text: Homer, stop picking at it. But I'm so sweet and tasty.
jokes.setup: Homer has donut permanently attached to head
jokes.punchline: He's eating himself and enjoying it
jokes.explanation: Disturbing but funny - Homer's gluttony extending to self-consumption. Good character work.

jokes.text: The 'School Bus'? Oh! Oh! They must mean the 'Ghoul Bus.' Nope. It says right there, 'School Bus.' Well, there's nothing scarier than having to go to school.
jokes.setup: Reading the title of the next horror segment
jokes.punchline: Lisa's matter-of-fact observation that regular school is the real horror
jokes.explanation: Relatable humor about school being genuinely frightening to kids, with meta-commentary on the segment title

jokes.text: Oh! Oh! They must mean the 'Ghoul Bus.' Nope. It says right there, 'School Bus.' Well, there's nothing scarier than having to go to school.
jokes.setup: Title card reading 'School Bus' for horror segment
jokes.punchline: Bart's matter-of-fact observation about school being genuinely terrifying
jokes.explanation: Universal childhood sentiment delivered by perfect character. Good observational humor.

jokes.text: Krusty poses for trading card photo. Hmm. He seems to be running a little low on ideas. Well, at least you got the gum. Oww! I cut my cheek!
jokes.setup: Boring Krusty trading card
jokes.punchline: Even the consolation prize (gum) is dangerous
jokes.explanation: Commentary on cheap trading card quality with physical payoff. Competent but not exceptional.

jokes.text: Oh-- I hope this is sweat.
jokes.setup: Otto experiencing something dripping on him while driving
jokes.punchline: Understated concern about bodily fluids
jokes.explanation: Gross-out humor with good economy. Fits Otto's laid-back character.

jokes.text: Hello, Simpson. I'm riding the bus because Mother hid my car keys... to punish me for talking to a woman on the phone. She was right to do it.
jokes.setup: Skinner explaining why he's on school bus
jokes.punchline: Self-flagellating about normal adult behavior
jokes.explanation: Perfect Skinner psychology - extreme mother issues with self-justification. Excellent character work.

jokes.text: Hey, where's your diaper, baby? Thank goodness he's drawn attention away from my shirt.
jokes.setup: Kids bullying Bart for his nightmare claims
jokes.punchline: Martin grateful for redirected mockery
jokes.explanation: Good Martin character moment - intellectual but still worried about social status.

jokes.text: If I lean over, I leave myself open to wedgies, wet willies... or even the dreaded rear admiral.
jokes.setup: Milhouse refusing to look out bus window
jokes.punchline: Escalating list of childhood tortures with naval terminology
jokes.explanation: Creative escalation with made-up bullying term. Good structure and Milhouse's paranoia.

jokes.text: No! Oh, no. I just made my last payment.
jokes.setup: Bus being damaged by gremlin
jokes.punchline: Otto's perfectly-timed financial irony
jokes.explanation: Classic timing for financial misfortune. Good economy and universal frustration.

jokes.text: Una momento, por favor. Now, I've gotten word that a child is using his imagination... and I've come to put a stop to it.
jokes.setup: Skinner stopping the bus
jokes.punchline: Treating imagination as a punishable offense
jokes.explanation: Brilliant authoritarian education satire. Skinner treating creativity as disruption - perfectly in character and quotable.

jokes.text: Ach. Me mule wouldn't walk in the mud. So I had to put 17 bullets in him.
jokes.setup: Willie sharing random backstory
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of extreme animal cruelty with specific detail
jokes.explanation: Dark Willie humor with absurd specificity. The '17 bullets' detail makes it absurd rather than cruel.

jokes.text: Oh, guten Tag! Would you care for a bite of my Vengelerstrasse bar? I also have a bag of marzipan JoyJoys!
jokes.setup: German exchange student being friendly
jokes.punchline: Over-the-top German candy names
jokes.explanation: Stereotypical German character humor with made-up candy names. Functional but predictable.

jokes.text: I'm doin' all the pullin', ya blouse-wearin' poodle walker!
jokes.setup: Willie and Skinner trying to steer runaway bus
jokes.punchline: Willie's creative insults for Skinner's effeminacy
jokes.explanation: Creative Willie insult combining two emasculating images. Good character voice.

jokes.text: Aw, isn't that cute? He's trying to claw my eyes out.
jokes.setup: Gremlin attacking Ned Flanders
jokes.punchline: Flanders' characteristic positive spin on violence
jokes.explanation: Perfect Flanders - finding the positive in attempted murder. Excellent character integration.

jokes.text: Right or wrong, your behavior was still disruptive, young man. Perhaps spending the remainder of your life in a madhouse... will teach you some manners.
jokes.setup: Bart proven right about gremlin
jokes.punchline: Skinner still punishing him for disruption regardless of truth
jokes.explanation: Brilliant authoritarian mindset - truth is irrelevant to maintaining order. Perfect Skinner psychology.

jokes.text: Hidilly-ho, Bart!
jokes.setup: Bart finally getting peace in asylum
jokes.punchline: Flanders appearing as his roommate
jokes.explanation: Good story-ending twist - no escape from Flanders. Efficient nightmare scenario.

jokes.text: They're dogs, and they're playing poker!
jokes.setup: Narrator describing most terrifying painting
jokes.punchline: Famous kitsch artwork presented as horror
jokes.explanation: Meta-joke about tacky art. Good economy but limited character integration.

jokes.text: We had a story to go with this painting, but it was far too intense. So we just threw something together with vampires. Enjoy!
jokes.setup: Transitioning to vampire segment
jokes.punchline: Admitting they phoned in the final story
jokes.explanation: Self-deprecating meta-humor about writing process. Honest and economical.

jokes.text: We think we're dealing with a supernatural being... most likely a mummy. As a precaution, I've ordered the Egyptian wing of the Springfield Museum destroyed.
jokes.setup: Police investigating vampire attacks
jokes.punchline: Completely wrong conclusion leading to cultural destruction
jokes.explanation: Good police incompetence humor with escalating consequences. Well-structured absurdist logic.

jokes.text: Lisa, vampires are make-believe. Just like elves, gremlins and Eskimos.
jokes.setup: Lisa warning about vampires
jokes.punchline: Homer's casual racism/ignorance mixed with fantasy creatures
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer ignorance - casually denying the existence of real people. Dark but character-appropriate.

jokes.text: What? Oh. Precious blood.
jokes.setup: Burns being interviewed about blood bank purchase
jokes.punchline: Accidentally revealing his vampire nature on TV
jokes.explanation: Good Burns moment - his evil nature slipping out. Economical delivery.

jokes.text: Ah, fresh victims for my ever-growing army of the undead. Sir, you have to let go of the button. Oh, son of a bit--
jokes.setup: Burns greeting the Simpsons at his mansion
jokes.punchline: Accidentally broadcasting evil plans via intercom
jokes.explanation: Classic Burns incompetence with technology. Good structure with Smithers assist.

jokes.text: Yeah, his hairdo looks so queer. I heard that! It was the boy!
jokes.setup: Homer commenting on Burns' appearance
jokes.punchline: Burns hearing the insult and Homer throwing Bart under the bus
jokes.explanation: Homer's cowardice and bad parenting in one moment. Good character work but dated language.

jokes.text: Dad, this is blood. Correction-- free blood.
jokes.setup: Lisa warning Homer about the punch
jokes.punchline: Homer's priorities focused on price rather than substance
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer economics - free overrides all other concerns. Excellent character integration.

jokes.text: Lisa, Burns isn't a vampire. And even if he was, we're not gonna stumble on his secret hiding place.
jokes.setup: Bart dismissing Lisa's vampire theory
jokes.punchline: Immediately discovering vampire coffins
jokes.explanation: Classic dramatic irony structure. Predictable but well-executed timing.

jokes.text: Big deal. It's no different from the basement in Grampa's rest home.
jokes.setup: Discovering vampire coffins
jokes.punchline: Comparing horror to nursing home normalcy
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about aging and death. Good economy and unexpected comparison.

jokes.text: Please, Bart. I've seen your stupid Shemp. Yeah, I've seen your Curly too.
jokes.setup: Bart doing Three Stooges impressions in vampire lair
jokes.punchline: Lisa's exasperated familiarity with his repertoire
jokes.explanation: Good sibling dynamic - Lisa's patience with Bart's antics wearing thin. Character-appropriate.

jokes.text: I know I really shouldn't, but when am I gonna be here again? Whee!
jokes.setup: Bart finding vampire coffin slide
jokes.punchline: Tourist mentality about supernatural danger
jokes.explanation: Perfect kid logic - treating vampire lair like amusement park. Good character work.

jokes.text: Hello, Mother. Hello, Father. I missed you during my uneventful absence.
jokes.setup: Bart returning as vampire
jokes.punchline: Formal speech and understated description of transformation
jokes.explanation: Good vampire transformation humor - overly formal Bart. Decent structure and economy.

jokes.text: Oh, Lisa, you and your stories. 'Bart is a vampire.' 'Beer kills brain cells.' Now, let's go back to that... building thingy... where our beds and TV... is.
jokes.setup: Homer dismissing Lisa's warnings
jokes.punchline: Struggling to remember word 'house' while dismissing intelligence claims
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer irony - denying brain damage while demonstrating it. Excellent character work and highly quotable.

jokes.text: Come join us, Lisa. It's so cool. You get to stay up all night drinking blood. And if you say you're a vampire... you get a free small soda at the movies.
jokes.setup: Vampire Bart trying to recruit Lisa
jokes.punchline: Mundane movie theater perk as selling point for vampirism
jokes.explanation: Brilliant juxtaposition of eternal damnation with minor consumer benefit. Great absurdist logic.

jokes.text: Homer, we gotta do something. Today, he's drinking people's blood. Tomorrow, he could be smoking.
jokes.setup: Marge worried about vampire Bart
jokes.punchline: More concerned about smoking than vampirism
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge priorities - parental concern about wrong thing. Brilliant escalation logic and highly quotable.

jokes.text: Kill my boss? Do I dare live out the American dream?
jokes.setup: Told he must kill Mr. Burns to save Bart
jokes.punchline: Framing boss murder as patriotic aspiration
jokes.explanation: Dark workplace humor with patriotic twist. Excellent Homer logic and very quotable.

jokes.text: Uh, Dad, that's his crotch. Oh, sorry.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to stake Burns through the heart
jokes.punchline: Missing the target completely
jokes.explanation: Simple physical comedy with Homer's incompetence. Functional but predictable.

jokes.text: You're fired! D'oh!
jokes.setup: Homer successfully killing Burns
jokes.punchline: Burns' final words and Homer's signature reaction
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moments - Burns' priorities and Homer's reaction. Great economy and callback integration.

jokes.text: I'm a vampire, and I've come to suck your blood. Blah! Oh-- This cape is giving me a rash.
jokes.setup: Grampa appearing as vampire
jokes.punchline: Mundane senior citizen complaint about costume
jokes.explanation: Good Grampa humor - even supernatural transformation can't overcome senior complaints.

jokes.text: You have to kill the head vampire. You're the head vampire? No, I'm the head vampire! Mom? I do have a life outside this house, you know.
jokes.setup: Trying to identify head vampire to kill
jokes.punchline: Revealing domestic Marge as ultimate evil
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion - wholesome Marge as head vampire. Her defensive response is perfectly in character.
